item.text: I’ll inspect the TLA+ module and existing lemmas, then iterate with TLAPS until the final theorem verifies.

item.aggregated_output:
-------------------------------- MODULE Sets_CardinalityOneConverse --------------------------------
EXTENDS Integers, NaturalsInduction, TLAPS
  \** NB: Module NaturalsInduction comes from the TLAPS library, usually
  \** installed in /usr/local/lib/tlaps. Make sure this is in your Toolbox
  \** search path, see Preferences/TLA+ Preferences.

IsBijection(f, S, T) == /\ f \in [S -> T]
                        /\ \A x, y \in S : (x # y) => (f[x] # f[y])
                        /\ \A y \in T : \E x \in S : f[x] = y


IsFiniteSet(S) == \E n \in Nat : \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)

(****************************************************************************)
(* Finite sets and cardinality are defined in the TLA+ standard module      *)
(* FiniteSets, but this is not yet natively supported by TLAPS. For the     *)
(* time being, we use the following axiom for defining set cardinality.     *)
(****************************************************************************)
\* Cardinality(S) == CHOOSE n : (n \in Nat) /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)

CONSTANT Cardinality(_)
AXIOM CardinalityAxiom ==
         \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) =>
           \A n : (n = Cardinality(S)) <=>
                    (n \in Nat) /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityInNat == \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => Cardinality(S) \in Nat
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityZero ==
           /\ IsFiniteSet({})
           /\ Cardinality({}) = 0
           /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ (Cardinality(S)=0) => (S = {})
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM CardinalityPlusOne ==
    ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
           NEW x, x \notin S
    PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup {x})
           /\ Cardinality(S \cup {x}) = Cardinality(S) + 1
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityOne == \A m : /\ IsFiniteSet({m})
                                 /\ Cardinality({m}) = 1
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM CardinalityTwo == \A m, p : m # p => 
                              /\ IsFiniteSet({m,p})
                              /\ Cardinality({m,p}) = 2
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IntervalCardinality ==  
  ASSUME NEW a \in Nat, NEW b \in Nat 
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(a..b)
         /\ Cardinality(a..b) = IF a > b THEN 0 ELSE b-a+1
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityOneConverse ==
   ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), Cardinality(S) = 1
   PROVE  \E m : S = {m}
PROOF OBVIOUS

=============================================================================

diff --git a/Sets_CardinalityOneConverse.tla b/Sets_CardinalityOneConverse.tla
index 840caa2..fe4d91e 100644
--- a/Sets_CardinalityOneConverse.tla
+++ b/Sets_CardinalityOneConverse.tla
@@ -65,6 +65,41 @@ THEOREM IntervalCardinality ==
 THEOREM CardinalityOneConverse ==
    ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), Cardinality(S) = 1
    PROVE  \E m : S = {m}
-PROOF OBVIOUS
-
-=============================================================================
\ No newline at end of file
+PROOF
+  <1>1. 1 = Cardinality(S)
+    OBVIOUS
+  <1>2. 1 \in Nat /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..1, S)
+    BY <1>1, CardinalityAxiom
+  <1>3. PICK f : IsBijection(f, 1..1, S)
+    BY <1>2
+  <1>4. f \in [1..1 -> S]
+    BY <1>3 DEF IsBijection
+  <1>5. 1 \in 1..1
+    BY SMT
+  <1>6. f[1] \in S
+    BY <1>4, <1>5
+  <1>7. S = {f[1]}
+  PROOF
+    <2>1. \A y : y \in S => y \in {f[1]}
+    PROOF
+      <3>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW y, y \in S
+             PROVE  y \in {f[1]}
+        OBVIOUS
+      <3>2. \E x \in 1..1 : f[x] = y
+        BY <1>3, <3>1 DEF IsBijection
+      <3>3. PICK x \in 1..1 : f[x] = y
+        BY <3>2
+      <3>4. x = 1
+        BY <3>3
+      <3>5. y = f[1]
+        BY <3>3, <3>4
+      <3>6. QED
+        BY <3>5
+    <2>2. \A y : y \in {f[1]} => y \in S
+      BY <1>6
+    <2>3. QED
+      BY <2>1, <2>2
+  <1>8. QED
+    BY <1>7
+
+=============================================================================
    60	         /\ Cardinality(a..b) = IF a > b THEN 0 ELSE b-a+1
    61	  PROOF OMITTED
    62	
    63	------------------------------------------------------------------
    64	
    65	THEOREM CardinalityOneConverse ==
    66	   ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), Cardinality(S) = 1
    67	   PROVE  \E m : S = {m}
    68	PROOF
    69	  <1>1. 1 = Cardinality(S)
    70	    OBVIOUS
    71	  <1>2. 1 \in Nat /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..1, S)
    72	    BY <1>1, CardinalityAxiom
    73	  <1>3. PICK f : IsBijection(f, 1..1, S)
    74	    BY <1>2
    75	  <1>4. f \in [1..1 -> S]
    76	    BY <1>3 DEF IsBijection
    77	  <1>5. 1 \in 1..1
    78	    BY SMT
    79	  <1>6. f[1] \in S
    80	    BY <1>4, <1>5
    81	  <1>7. S = {f[1]}
    82	  PROOF
    83	    <2>1. \A y : y \in S => y \in {f[1]}
    84	    PROOF
    85	      <3>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW y, y \in S
    86	             PROVE  y \in {f[1]}
    87	        OBVIOUS
    88	      <3>2. \E x \in 1..1 : f[x] = y
    89	        BY <1>3, <3>1 DEF IsBijection
    90	      <3>3. PICK x \in 1..1 : f[x] = y
    91	        BY <3>2
    92	      <3>4. x = 1
    93	        BY <3>3
    94	      <3>5. y = f[1]
    95	        BY <3>3, <3>4
    96	      <3>6. QED
    97	        BY <3>5
    98	    <2>2. \A y : y \in {f[1]} => y \in S
    99	      BY <1>6
   100	    <2>3. QED
   101	      BY <2>1, <2>2
   102	  <1>8. QED
   103	    BY <1>7
